name: Assagadors and Covatelles Summit Trail
name_original: Ruta dels Assagadors i Cim de Covatelles (PR-CV 347) — circular desde La Font d'en Carrós
description: Hike the moderate 3.9-mile Ruta dels Assagadors i Cim de Covatelles (PR-CV 347) for panoramic views and historic paths.
region_intro: Hiking around La Font D'En Carròs offers diverse landscapes, from serene river walks to mountainous terrain. The region features pine groves, ancient olive and carob tree crops, and elevated points providing views of the Serpis valley. Hikers can explore trails that traverse varied topography, including hills and natural features like the Sima Blanca. Best hiking trails around La Font D'En Carròs The most popular hiking route is Assagadors and Covatelles Summit Trail, a 3.9 miles (6.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 8 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers panoramic views and passes through historic paths and pine groves. Another top favourite among local hikers is Castell de Rebollet Trail (SL-CV 60), a difficult 2.1 miles (3.4 km) path. This trail explores historic castle ruins and provides panoramic views of the Serpis valley and the Mediterranean Sea. Local hikers also love the Covatelles Summit – Views from the trail loop from la Font d'en Carròs, a 8.5 miles (13.7 km) trail leading through varied terrain with significant elevation gain, often completed in about 4 hours 25 minutes. Hiking around La Font D'En Carròs is defined by mountainous terrain, river valleys, and historic landmarks, including castle ruins. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more difficult ascents. The routes in La Font D'En Carròs are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 400 reviews. More than 2000 hikers have used komoot to explore La Font D'En Carròs's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in La Font D'En Carròs?
A: There are over 20 hiking routes around La Font D'En Carròs, offering a variety of experiences from moderate paths to more challenging ascents. The komoot community has mapped 23 tours in total.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around La Font D'En Carròs?
A: The region offers diverse landscapes, ranging from serene river walks to mountainous terrain. You'll find trails traversing pine groves, ancient olive and carob tree crops, and elevated points providing breathtaking views of the Serpis valley and the Mediterranean Sea. Expect varied topography, including hills and natural features like the Sima Blanca.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in La Font D'En Carròs?
A: Yes, while many routes are moderate, there is at least one easy trail available. For those seeking flatter routes, the Ruta dels Molins is a good option, allowing visitors to discover the water heritage of La Font D'En Carròs. It's suitable for walking or cycling.

Q: Can I find longer, more challenging hikes in the area?
A: Absolutely. For a longer, moderate challenge, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31454723" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covatelles Summit – Views from the trail loop from la Font d'en Carròs&lt;/a&gt;, which is 8.5 miles (13.7 km) and typically takes around 4 hours 25 minutes to complete. If you're looking for a difficult ascent with historical interest, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1353830220/castell-de-rebollet-trail-sl-cv-60" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castell de Rebollet Trail (SL-CV 60)&lt;/a&gt; offers panoramic views from historic castle ruins.

Q: What natural landmarks or viewpoints can I see while hiking?
A: Hikers can enjoy privileged views of the Serpis valley from elevated points like the Rebollet Castle. The region also features the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/349459" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covatelles Summit&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6236363" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molló de la Falconera&lt;/a&gt;, both offering stunning vistas. The Sima Blanca, a tectonic cave, is a notable natural feature along the SL-CV 60 path, with great surrounding views.

Q: Are there any historical sites to explore on the trails?
A: Yes, the area is rich in history. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1353830220/castell-de-rebollet-trail-sl-cv-60" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castell de Rebollet Trail (SL-CV 60)&lt;/a&gt; leads to the remains of the 16th-century Rebollet Castle, with findings dating back to the Bronze Age. The town center itself features the 14th-century Sant Antoni Màrtir Parochial Church and devotional ceramic panels.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in La Font D'En Carròs?
A: Yes, several routes offer circular paths. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31454723" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covatelles Summit – Views from the trail loop from la Font d'en Carròs&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular option. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5300351" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sima Blanca – Covatelles Summit loop from Bella Vista&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 4.6-mile (7.5 km) hike.

Q: Are the hiking trails in La Font D'En Carròs suitable for families with children?
A: Many trails are suitable for families, especially those seeking moderate walks. The Parc Públic Municipal in town is a family-friendly destination with playgrounds, and the Parc de la Font Gran offers picturesque picnic spots. When choosing a trail, consider the difficulty and duration to match your family's capabilities.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in La Font D'En Carròs?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 400 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from serene river walks to mountainous terrain, and the panoramic views of the Serpis valley and the Mediterranean Sea. The well-marked trails and historical landmarks like Rebollet Castle are also frequently highlighted.

Q: What is the best time of year for hiking in La Font D'En Carròs?
A: The Valencian Community generally enjoys a mild Mediterranean climate, making hiking pleasant for much of the year. Spring and autumn are often ideal, offering comfortable temperatures and lush scenery. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended, while winters are generally mild enough for outdoor activities.

Q: Are there any lakes or natural springs near the hiking routes?
A: Yes, the region features several interesting water bodies. You can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5152261" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Salty Spring&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4866511" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural area of the "Font Salà"&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5968459" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Duke's Pond Spring&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5968461" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;L'Ullal of the Marjal de Gandia&lt;/a&gt; are also notable natural monuments in the vicinity.

Q: Is there parking available at the trailheads?
A: While specific trailhead parking information isn't always detailed for every route, La Font D'En Carròs is a town that serves as a starting point for many trails. It's generally advisable to look for public parking within the town or at designated areas near popular trail access points. Always check local signage for parking regulations.
tips: The PR-CV 347 trail, known as Ruta dels Assagadors and Cim de Covatelles, is a beautiful trail located in the Font d'en Carròs.
This part of the trail on route PR-CV 347 is full of large rocks, so be very careful, especially after rain or during periods of high humidity. Enjoy the lush vegetation and shade.
The PR-CV 347 trail is a route with great scenic value. Enjoy the experience on this perfectly marked trail.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the Ruta dels Assagadors i Cim de Covatelles?
faqs.answer: The trail is circular and originates from La Font d'en Carrós. You can typically find parking within the town, which serves as the trailhead for this route.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The Ruta dels Assagadors i Cim de Covatelles is rated as moderate. It involves sustained ascents and descents, and varied terrain including irregular and stony paths. Hikers should have a reasonable level of physical fitness and some experience.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or views along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers several notable features. You'll traverse historic 'assagadors' (livestock paths with dry stone walls), encounter ancient stone boundary markers, and pass by rocky trail sections. A major highlight is the Covatelles Summit, which provides splendid views of the La Safor region and the Mediterranean coast, extending from Cullera to Cap de Sant Antoni and Montgó. You can also observe the Circ de la Safor, Benicadell, and Montdúver mountain ranges inland. There's also a site called La Mola, featuring a large stone believed to be an abandoned millstone.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular hike?
faqs.answer: This circular hike typically takes around 2 hours and 7 minutes to complete, though this can vary based on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the views.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail passes through natural areas, there is no specific information prohibiting dogs. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially given the presence of historic livestock paths ('assagadors') and potential wildlife. Please practice Leave No Trace principles.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike the PR-CV 347?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for hiking. During these seasons, you might also experience vibrant flora. Summer can be very hot, so an early morning start is advisable if hiking then. Winter hiking is possible, but be prepared for cooler temperatures.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Ruta dels Assagadors i Cim de Covatelles?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied. You'll start with some concrete paths during the initial ascent, then transition to trails winding through thick pine forests and dry farming terraces. A significant portion follows 'assagadors' (historic livestock paths) often delimited by dry stone walls. As you ascend towards the summit, the vegetation shifts to more scrubland. The descent includes a firewall section and connections with other paths.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to hike the Ruta dels Assagadors i Cim de Covatelles (PR-CV 347). It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route partially overlaps with El Camí IT5TR5 El Camí del Sud: de Dénia a Castelló de la Plana for about 54% of its length.

faqs.question: What should I pack for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and varied terrain, it's recommended to pack plenty of water, snacks, sturdy hiking shoes, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and layers of clothing. A map or GPS device is also useful, though the trail is generally well-marked. Trekking poles can be helpful for the ascents and descents.

faqs.question: Are there any opportunities for a longer hike or detours?
faqs.answer: Yes, there is a possible detour to El Cati mentioned along the route, which could extend your hiking experience. Additionally, the trail connects with other paths like the Camí dels Jugadors, offering potential for exploration if you wish to extend your adventure.
